info.description:
This is Humand Public API's Documentation. To use this api you will have to send an api key in the Authorization HTTP header as follows: 'Basic {{api_key}}'

## Rate Limiting

The API implements rate limiting to ensure fair usage and protect the platform. If you exceed the rate limit, you will receive a `429 Too Many Requests` response.

All responses include the following headers to help you monitor your API usage:

- **RateLimit-Policy**: Describes the rate limit policy applied. Format: `{limit};w={window_in_seconds}`

  Example: `50;w=60` (50 requests per 60-second window)

- **RateLimit**: Shows your current rate limit status. Format: `limit={max}, remaining={remaining}, reset={seconds_until_reset}`

  Example: `limit=50, remaining=45, reset=30`

- **Retry-After**: (only included in 429 responses) Number of seconds to wait before retrying
info.title: Humand Public API

get.description: List users, including searching and pagination

post.description: Creates a new user

put.description:
Update an existing user or create a new one if it does not exist. When updating, relationships use merge-by-type semantics:
- Sending `BOSS` entries (or `[]`) updates/clears org-chart bosses and reviewer; sending only `REVIEWER` entries leaves existing bosses untouched.
- Set `employeeInternalId` to `null` to remove that relationship type.
- A user cannot be their own reviewer.

requestBody.description: User object to be updated or created.

- Set `employeeInternalId` to `null` to remove that relationship type.
- A user cannot be their own reviewer.

get.description: List paginated time off requests with filters

post.summary: Manual correction or overwrite of time off balances
post.description:
Allows manual correction of time off balances using three operations:
- ADDITION: Adds amount to current balance (incremental)
- SUBTRACTION: Subtracts amount from current balance (incremental)
- SET: Sets balance to specified amount (overwrites current balance)

For SET operation:
- BASIC_ANNUAL policies: Sets available balance
- UNLIMITED policies: Sets days used

The accreditationYear parameter is optional. If not provided, uses current cycle.

put.description: Update the state of a Time Off Request. Supports rejecting, approving, or cancelling requests. When approving, all pending approval steps are automatically approved. For cancellation, only approved requests can be cancelled and the balance will be reverted.

approved.description: Approve a time-off request, automatically approving all pending steps

cancelled.description: Cancel an approved time-off request. The balance of the approved request will be reverted.

put.description: Update the start and end dates of a Time Off Request. Optionally, you can also update the consumption types (FULL_DAY or HALF_DAY) for the start and end dates. If consumption types are not provided, the existing values will be preserved. The request must not be fully approved. Follows the same validation rules as creating a new request.

updateDatesOnly.description: Update only the start and end dates of a time-off request, keeping existing consumption types

updateDatesWithConsumption.description: Update dates and set specific consumption types for both start and end dates

changeToHalfDays.description: Update dates and change both dates to half-day consumption

changeOnlyEndDate.description: Update dates and change only the end date consumption type

post.description:
Asynchronously enqueues the creation of multiple Time Off Requests for the given policy. Returns a `jobId` immediately; the batch is processed in the background.

Use `GET /time-off/requests/bulk/{id}` (see below) to retrieve the job status and per-row errors. That GET is the **complement** of this POST — not a general-purpose Jobs API. The caller decides if and when to fetch the status (one-shot, polled, or not at all). There is no forced polling cadence.

Per-row errors (missing fields, unknown employee, bad date format, comment too long, duplicated row, blocked-dates conflict, etc.) are reported in the `errors` array of the GET response. Validations bypassed for admin-on-behalf creation (overlapping requests, future/retroactive, AR/BR law, balance) do not surface here — this matches the semantics of the admin XLSX bulk endpoint.

get.summary: Get Bulk Time Off Requests Creation Job
get.description:
Returns the status and per-row errors of a job created by `POST /time-off/policies/{id}/requests/bulk`. This endpoint is the **complement** of that POST — not a general-purpose Jobs API. Any job whose type is not `BULK_TIME_OFF_REQUESTS_CREATION`, or whose instance does not match the caller's, returns 404.

The caller chooses if and when to invoke this endpoint — there is no forced polling. We recommend an exponential backoff starting around 5–10 seconds for long batches.
List paginated time tracking day summaries
List paginated time tracking requests entries

get.description: Returns a paginated day-by-day calendar of assigned shifts for a given employee within an optional date range. Each page item is one day (with an empty shifts array when the employee has no assignment that day). When dateFrom or dateTo are not provided, both default to today.

parameters.name: dateFrom
parameters.description: Start date (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to dateTo or today.

parameters.name: dateTo
parameters.description: End date (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to dateFrom or today.

post.description:
Generates a detailed report of all course tasks for a specific user and sends it via email.
The report includes:
- User information (ID, activation date, job positions)
- Segmentation groups and their values
- All course tasks with their status, dates, duration, and grades
- Learning path associations

200.description: Report generation initiated successfully. The report will be sent to the provided email.

get.description:
Returns paginated progress data for all users enrolled in a course.
Each item includes user data, segmentation groups, course progress,
and a per-task breakdown with status and grade.

parameters.name: groupIds
parameters.description: Comma-separated segmentation group IDs to include in the response

parameters.name: userFields
parameters.description: Comma-separated user profile field keys to include (e.g. COMPLETE_NAME, EMPLOYEE_INTERNAL_ID)

200.description: Paginated list of user progress for the course

Create or update multiple knowledge libraries in a single atomic request. Items without an `id` will be created; items with an `id` will be updated. Maximum 50 items per request. The entire operation is atomic — if any item fails, all changes are rolled back.

message.example: Item at index 3: Knowledge Library with id 999 not found

post.summary: Create the same goal for multiple users

post.description: Generates a new API key and invalidates the current one. Store the new key securely — it cannot be retrieved again.
Permanently invalidates the current API key. This action is irreversible. A new key must be generated to regain access.

get.summary: Get IP allowlist for the current API key
get.description: Returns the list of allowed IP addresses and CIDR ranges configured for the current API key. If the list is empty, requests from any IP are allowed.

put.summary: Update IP allowlist for the current API key
put.description: Sets the list of allowed IP addresses and CIDR ranges for the current API key. Once configured, only requests from the listed IPs will be accepted. Send an empty array to remove all IP restrictions. Your current IP must be included in the allowlist to prevent accidental lockout. Supports both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses and CIDR notation (e.g. 10.0.0.0/24, 2001:db8::/32). Maximum 50 entries.

403.description: Your current IP is not included in the new allowlist. Include your IP to prevent lockout.

delete.summary: Remove IP allowlist for the current API key
delete.description: Removes all IP restrictions from the current API key, allowing requests from any IP address. This is equivalent to updating the allowlist with an empty array.

Unread.name: unread
Unread.description: To filter unread chats or messages (depending on endpoint). If false, return all chats/messages

ReceiverEmployeeInternalId.name: receiverEmployeeInternalId
ReceiverEmployeeInternalId.description: A receiver is used for chats and messages. A receiver for chats lets you filter all chats where the receiver belongs to, meanwhile a receiver for messages lets you filter all messages sent to the receiver.

Public.name: public
Public.description: Whether if the file must be public or not

Order.name: order
Order.description: Used to select the sort type for response columns (asc o desc)

CreationDate.name: creationDate
CreationDate.description: Used to get results by creation date

TimeOffRequestStates.name: states
TimeOffRequestStates.description: Used to get results by states

PolicyTypeIds.name: policyTypeIds
PolicyTypeIds.description: Used to get results by policy type ids

PolicyIds.name: policyIds
PolicyIds.description: Used to get results by policy ids

TimeOffFromDate.name: fromDate
TimeOffFromDate.description: Retrieve results based on a starting date. Includes all requests with days from this date onwards.

TimeOffToDate.name: toDate
TimeOffToDate.description: Retrieve results based on and end date. Includes all requests up to and including this date.

TimeOffResolutionFromDate.name: resolutionFromDate
TimeOffResolutionFromDate.description: Retrieve results for which the review process ended after this date. Includes all requests with resolution date from this date onwards.

TimeOffResolutionToDate.name: resolutionToDate
TimeOffResolutionToDate.description: Retrieve results for which the review process before after this date. Includes all requests with resolution date up to and including this date.

TimeOffCreatedAtSince.name: createdAtSince
TimeOffCreatedAtSince.description: Retrieve results created on or after this date. Includes all requests with creation date from this date onwards.

TimeOffUserId.name: userId
TimeOffUserId.description: Filter requests by the internal user ID of the issuer.

TimeOffEmployeeInternalId.name: employeeInternalId
TimeOffEmployeeInternalId.description: Filter requests by the employee internal ID of the issuer.

TimeOffRequestId.name: requestId
TimeOffRequestId.description: Filter requests by the request ID

TimeTrackingEntryStartDate.name: startDate
TimeTrackingEntryStartDate.description: Start date (inclusive) to filter time tracking entries. Only entries with a timestamp on or after this date will be retrieved.

TimeTrackingEntryEndDate.name: endDate
TimeTrackingEntryEndDate.description: End date (inclusive) to filter time tracking entries. Only entries with a timestamp up to and including this date will be retrieved.

password.description: Only used for creating users, not when updating one.

operation.description:
- ADDITION: Adds amount to current balance
- SUBTRACTION: Subtracts amount from current balance
- SET: Sets balance to the specified amount (overwrites current balance)

amount.description:
For ADDITION/SUBTRACTION: amount to add/subtract (must be positive).
For SET: target balance value (can be positive, negative, or zero).

observations.description: Optional observations for the balance correction

accreditationYear.description:
Optional. Year of the cycle to apply the correction.
If not specified, uses the current cycle.

fromDate.description: Start date of the time-off request

toDate.description: End date of the time-off request

fromConsumptionType.description: Optional. Consumption type for the start date (HALF_DAY or FULL_DAY). If not provided, keeps the existing value.

toConsumptionType.description: Optional. Consumption type for the end date (HALF_DAY or FULL_DAY). If not provided, keeps the existing value.

employeeInternalId.description: Internal id of the employee for whom the request is being created.

comment.description: Optional free-text comment, up to 255 characters.

items.description: Array of items to create. Each item is processed independently; per-row errors do not abort the batch.

jobId.description: Id of the asynchronous job that will process the batch. Use it with `GET /time-off/requests/bulk/{id}` to retrieve status and per-row errors.

status.description: Initial job status. Always PENDING immediately after enqueueing.

totalRows.description: Number of items submitted in the request.

rowNumber.description: 1-indexed row number of the failing item, or null if the error is not attributable to a specific row.

code.description:
Error code. Common values for this endpoint include:
- `MISSING_EMPLOYEE_INTERNAL_ID`
- `NOT_EXIST_EMPLOYEE_INTERNAL_ID`
- `MISSING_START_DATE`
- `MISSING_END_DATE`
- `BAD_START_DATE`
- `BAD_END_DATE`
- `BAD_START_DATE_END_DATE_ORDER`
- `COMMENT_TOO_LONG`
- `DUPLICATED_ROW`

processedRows.description: Number of items processed so far. Equals totalRows when the job is in a terminal state.

errors.description: Per-row errors collected during processing. Empty if no rows failed.

newBossId.description: Optional. Id of the user that will become the boss of the deactivated user's subordinates. If not provided, the existing boss is used.

deactivationObservation.description: Optional. Free-text observation about the deactivation.
deactivationObservation.example: Employee left the company on good terms.

employeeInternalId.description: The employeeInternalId of the related user. Set to null to remove this relationship type.

signatureCoordinates.description: PDF coordinates where the signature should be placed. Specifies the position and dimensions of a single signature field on a specific page.
PDF coordinates where the signature should be placed. Specifies the position and dimensions of a single signature field on a specific page.

creationUrl.description: The URL used to create the signed resource.

amountInTime.description: Days requested in time (not sold for money). Calculated as amountRequested - amountInMoney.

amountInMoney.description: Days sold for money. Null or 0 if balance sale is not used.

documentation.description: Array of URLs for attached documents. Empty or omitted if no documents are attached.

now.description: The time for creating the entry. Can be past but not future

hasSchedule.description: Whether the employee has a shift or work schedule assigned for this day (true even if it is a rest day within the schedule).

TimeTrackingDaySummaryHours.description: Consolidated hour counters for the day

employees.description: List of employees and their shift assignments. Maximum 100 employees per request.

days.description: List of days with shift assignments. Maximum 30 days per employee.

timeSlots.description: List of time slots for the day. Each time slot is an array with start and end times in HH:mm format.

isWorkday.description: Indicates if this is a working day

skipped.description: Shift assignments that were skipped with the reason

isWorkday.description: Indicates if this is a working day

timeSlots.description: List of time slots for the day

PublicApiShiftInfo.description: Composite shift object with metadata and all time slots

templateId.description: ID of the associated shift template, or null when the shift was not assigned from the template repository

shifts.description: Shifts assigned to the employee for this date

employeeInternalId.description: Employee internal ID of the member

items.description: Employee internal ID of the member

id.description: If provided, the item is treated as an update. Must belong to the same instance. Send null or omit to create.

parentId.description: Parent knowledge library ID. Use null for root level. Only used on create; ignored on update.

employeeInternalId.description: Employee internal ID of the member

items.description: Employee internal ID of the member

terminal.description: Whether this is a terminal state

taskType.description: Type of task for this state

fillingTime.description: Time taken to fill the form

pdfFileAssetUrl.description: URL of the PDF file asset associated with the form answer

pictureUrl.description: The url of the course picture, use temporal file upload endpoint to upload the picture (/signed-url)

url.description: Use temporal file upload endpoint to upload the file (/signed-url) previously to use this field

employeeInternalId.description: The employee internal ID of the user to generate the report for

email.description: The email address where the report will be sent

userFields.description: Key-value map of requested user profile fields

pictureId.description: ID of the cover picture uploaded via /learning-sessions/cover-picture

isObligatory.description: Whether the learning session is mandatory or optional

issueCertificate.description: Whether to issue a certificate upon completion

status.description: Set to DRAFT to create a session in draft state. If not provided, the session will be published immediately.

isObligatory.description: Whether the learning session is mandatory or optional

pictureId.description: ID of the cover picture uploaded via /learning-sessions/cover-picture

apiKey.description: The new API key. Store it securely — it cannot be retrieved again.

keyId.description: Stable identifier of the new API key. Use it on subsequent management endpoints.

UnauthorizedError.description: API Key is missing or invalid

ForbiddenError.description: You are not allowed to access this resource

RolesConflictError.description: Cannot edit segmentation group because it has roles related to the items

message.default: Cannot delete segmentation item related with roles
Rate limit reached, please try again later

RateLimit-Policy.description: Describes the rate limit policy applied. Format: {limit};w={window_in_seconds}. Example: 50;w=60 means 50 requests per 60 second window

RateLimit.description: Current rate limit status. Format: limit={max}, remaining={remaining}, reset={seconds_until_reset}. Example: limit=50, remaining=0, reset=42

Retry-After.description: Number of seconds to wait before making another request
